caption:
The Indian government has begun working on plans to bring Formula 1 back to the country. Officials recently visited the Buddh International Circuit in Greater Noida, signaling strong interest in reviving the Indian Grand Prix.

The race was last held in 2013 but was dropped due to tax and regulatory issues. Now, with renewed government support, talks are underway to make India a part of the Formula 1 calendar once again.

If successful, this would mark a major comeback for motorsport in India after more than a decade, giving fans hope of seeing the world’s fastest cars on Indian soil once more.
.
.

caption:
Lewis Hamilton and Kim Kardashian are making headlines after being spotted together in Oxfordshire over the weekend. Their private getaway quickly sparked rumors of a possible romance.

Just days later, the two were seen arriving at a Paris hotel, adding fuel to the speculation. While neither has confirmed the relationship, fans and media outlets are calling this one of the most surprising pairings of 2026.
.

caption:
After years of recovery following his tragic 2013 ski accident, Michael Schumacher is reportedly no longer bedridden. Schumacher continues to receive 24-hour care from his wife Corinna and a dedicated team of nurses and therapists, with costs running into tens of thousands weekly. Only a small inner circle of trusted friends, including former Ferrari boss Jean Todt, are allowed to visit him.

His family has fiercely protected his privacy over the years, even pursuing legal action against those who attempted to exploit his condition through stolen medical records or unauthorized photos. While challenges remain, this latest update offers cautious optimism for fans worldwide who have long awaited news of Schumacher’s recovery.

.
.

caption:
Indian-Origin F1 driver Arvid Lindblad driving Sebastian Vettel’s World Championship winning Red Bull Racing RB8 - in VCARB colours. 

The legendary RB8 won the F1 Indian Grand Prix in Delhi NCR back in 2012, en route to winning a historic World Driver’s and Constructors Championship double.

With its naturally aspirated 2.4l V8 engine, it is bound to shake the ground and melt hearts as it fires up once again in Delhi NCR after 14 years!
